concepts.title: σ-algebras and measures
concepts.blurb: σ-algebras, countably additive measures, and the Borel σ-algebra generated by the open sets of R.

concepts.title: Outer measure & Carathéodory's criterion
concepts.blurb: Extend a premeasure on an algebra to an outer measure on all subsets; the Carathéodory additivity test picks out the σ-algebra on which the extension restricts to a countably additive measure.

concepts.title: Lebesgue measure
concepts.blurb: Apply Carathéodory to the length premeasure on intervals to get a translation-invariant complete measure on R; null sets and outer/inner regularity; non-measurable Vitali sets.

concepts.title: Measurable functions
concepts.blurb: f measurable iff preimages of Borel sets are measurable; simple functions approximate measurable functions from below.

concepts.title: Lebesgue integral
concepts.blurb: Integrate by partitioning the range, not the domain; agrees with Riemann when Riemann exists, but handles far more functions.

concepts.title: Monotone and dominated convergence
concepts.blurb: MCT and DCT let you swap limit and integral under mild hypotheses — the machine behind most measure-theoretic arguments.

concepts.title: Lᵖ spaces
concepts.blurb: Banach spaces of p-th-power integrable functions, Hölder and Minkowski, L² as the canonical Hilbert space of analysis.

concepts.title: Product measures & σ-finiteness
concepts.blurb: On σ-finite spaces the product σ-algebra carries a unique product measure satisfying μ⊗ν(A×B)=μ(A)ν(B); σ-finiteness is exactly what rescues uniqueness in Carathéodory's extension.

concepts.title: Fubini & Tonelli
concepts.blurb: Tonelli swaps iterated integrals for nonnegative measurable f (no integrability needed); Fubini extends this to complex-valued f ∈ L¹(μ⊗ν). Without absolute integrability the iterated integrals can disagree.

concepts.title: Signed measures & Radon–Nikodym
concepts.blurb: Hahn/Jordan decomposition factors a signed measure into positive and negative parts; Radon–Nikodym then writes any ν ≪ μ as ν = ∫f dμ for a μ-a.e. unique density f = dν/dμ ∈ L¹(μ).

concepts.title: Riesz representation (measure form)
concepts.blurb: Every positive linear functional Λ on C_c(X) for X locally compact Hausdorff has a unique regular Borel measure μ with Λ(f) = ∫f dμ — the bridge from functionals to measures.

concepts.title: Hausdorff measure & dimension
concepts.blurb: s-dimensional Hausdorff measure H^s counts size by Σ diam(U_i)^s over δ-covers; the unique s at which H^s jumps from ∞ to 0 is the Hausdorff dimension — the Cantor set has dim = log 2 / log 3.
